claims.claim_text: API prompt design commonly separates durable instructions into system or developer messages and task-specific details into user messages.
claims.span_hint: line 137 | section: Anatomy of a Production Prompt
claims.rationale: OpenAI's prompting guidance explicitly recommends putting overall tone or role guidance in the system message and task-specific details in user messages.

claims.claim_text: Prompt injection happens when untrusted text or data is treated like instructions and attempts to override intended model behavior.
claims.span_hint: line 154 | section: Anatomy of a Production Prompt
claims.rationale: OpenAI's agent safety guidance directly defines prompt injection in terms of untrusted text or data attempting to override instructions.

claims.claim_text: OpenAI's reasoning-model guidance says these models reason internally, work best with straightforward prompts, and often do not benefit from explicit 'think step by step' instructions.
claims.span_hint: line 231 | section: Choosing the Right Prompting Technique
claims.rationale: The reasoning best-practices guide explicitly says reasoning models perform best with straightforward prompts and that 'think step by step' prompting may not help.

claims.claim_text: Structured Outputs are recommended over plain JSON mode when supported because they enforce schema adherence rather than only valid JSON.
claims.span_hint: line 235 | section: Choosing the Right Prompting Technique
claims.rationale: The Structured Outputs guide directly contrasts JSON mode with schema-enforced Structured Outputs and recommends Structured Outputs when possible.

claims.claim_text: OWASP distinguishes direct prompt injection from indirect prompt injection embedded in external content such as web pages or files.
claims.span_hint: line 482 | section: Prompt Security, Boundaries, and Production Operations
claims.rationale: OWASP LLM01 explicitly defines prompt injection and discusses both direct and indirect forms.

claims.claim_text: The GPT-3 paper showed that prompt examples could guide behavior without task-specific fine-tuning.
claims.rationale: Brown et al. is the primary source for GPT-3's few-shot, text-only task adaptation framing.

claims.claim_text: OWASP classifies prompt injection as a major LLM application risk.
claims.rationale: OWASP's GenAI Top 10 lists prompt injection as LLM01:2025.

claims.claim_text: Structured outputs and local validation move part of prompt reliability from wording alone into testable interface contracts.
claims.rationale: The Structured Outputs docs explicitly say schema enforcement should be handled with Structured Outputs or, if unavailable, validation libraries and retries.

further_reading.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
further_reading.why_relevant: Foundational paper for in-context learning and few-shot prompting.

further_reading.title: OWASP LLM01:2025 Prompt Injection
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary security reference for prompt-injection terminology, threat models, and mitigations.

further_reading.title: The Prompt Report: A Systematic Survey of Prompting Techniques
further_reading.why_relevant: Broad survey of prompting techniques and terminology that complements the module's conceptual overview.

notes: Excluded hypothetical teaching scenarios, code blocks, quiz material, and exercise instructions. Focused on hard claims about message-role guidance, reasoning-model prompting, structured outputs, GPT-3 few-shot behavior, and prompt-injection security.
